sense.definition: a game where a small rubber ball is attached to a wooden paddle with an elastic string, and players hit the ball with the paddle to keep it in motion

examples.x: I spent the afternoon playing paddleball with my friends at the beach.
She brought a paddleball set to the park and we took turns hitting the ball back and forth.
He was really good at paddleball and kept the game going without missing a hit.
After the picnic, the kids started playing paddleball while the adults relaxed.
They were having so much fun with paddleball that they lost track of time.

sense.definition: a popular playground game where players throw a ball against a wall, and the other players must catch it on the rebound or face penalties

examples.x: Let's play wall ball after school; it's a great way to get some exercise.
I threw the ball too hard in wall ball, and it bounced right over my head.
We spent the afternoon playing wall ball in the park, trying to keep the ball from hitting the ground.
We need a bigger space to play wall ball because the wall here is too small.
Wall ball is a fun game to play when you don't have a lot of equipment or space.

sense.definition: a game where players throw a ball to each other and try to catch it without letting it fall
a game where one player or a team tries to keep possession of an object, such as a ball, while other players or teams attempt to take it away
a game in which two teams of players form circles and aim to hit their opponents with a ball in order to eliminate them while avoiding getting hit themselves

examples.x: We played dodgeball at recess today, and I was the last one standing on my team.
The gym teacher set up a dodgeball tournament, and all the classes participated.
I don’t like playing dodgeball because I always get hit right away.
After a few rounds of dodgeball, we were all out of breath from running around.

sense.definition: a fast-paced, dodgeball-like game played in an octagonal pit where players try to hit opponents below the knees while avoiding being hit themselves
a game where players throw a ball into a series of funnels, trying to get it through the smallest one to score points

examples.x: We played funnel ball at the park yesterday, and it was so much fun.
The kids spent hours playing funnel ball during the picnic.
She made an amazing shot in funnel ball, getting the ball through the funnel on her first try.
We set up a funnel ball game for the party, and everyone loved it.
I think funnel ball is a great game to play during family gatherings or outdoor events.

sense.definition: a game where teams or individuals compete in a relay race while passing a beach ball to one another

examples.x: We played a fun beach ball relay at the picnic, and everyone was laughing the whole time.
The kids were so excited when the teacher announced a beach ball relay for the school sports day.
During our family reunion, we organized a beach ball relay to keep the kids entertained.
The beach ball relay was a huge hit at the summer camp, with everyone cheering for their teams.
For our team-building event, we did a beach ball relay to see who could finish the fastest.

sense.definition: a game that involves using pool noodles as hockey sticks to hit a ball or puck, played similarly to ice or floor hockey

examples.x: We played pool noodle hockey at the beach, and it was so much fun!
The kids had a blast playing pool noodle hockey at the summer camp.
Pool noodle hockey is a great way to stay active and have fun with friends.
I’ve never played pool noodle hockey before, but it looks like a fun way to pass the time.
The pool noodle hockey match was competitive, but everyone was laughing the whole time.

sense.definition: a game that combines elements of soccer and golf, where players kick a soccer ball into a series of holes on a course using the fewest number of kicks possible

examples.x: We spent the afternoon playing soccer golf at the park, and it was a lot of fun.
The kids love soccer golf because it combines soccer with a bit of a challenge.
Soccer golf is perfect for a sunny day when you want to be outside but not run too much.
I’ve been practicing my accuracy for soccer golf, trying to get the ball in the hole with fewer kicks.
After the soccer golf game, we grabbed some drinks and sat by the course to relax.

sense.definition: a type of bowling game using smaller and lighter pins and balls compared to traditional tenpin bowling
a children's game in which players throw a ball in the air and must avoid being hit by it, or they receive a letter toward the word "spud"
an outdoor game played with a tall pole and a ball attached to a rope, where players hit the ball in opposite directions to wrap the rope around the pole in their favor

examples.x: The children at the playground took turns playing tetherball, swatting the ball around the pole in an attempt to wrap the rope fully around it.
Tetherball tournaments were a popular activity at summer camps, with campers competing for bragging rights and prizes.
He enjoyed the simple pleasure of playing tetherball with friends during recess, relishing the friendly competition and camaraderie.
The backyard barbecue featured a tetherball setup, providing entertainment for guests of all ages to enjoy between meals.

sense.definition: an ancient Chinese ball game where players kick a ball into a goal without using their hands, considered a forerunner of modern soccer

examples.x: The rules of cuju were much simpler than those of modern football.
He studied the history of cuju to understand how modern soccer developed.
The idea of cuju might have inspired similar ball games in other cultures.

sense.definition: a traditional Japanese game similar to badminton, played with wooden paddles and a shuttlecock made of feathers or plastic

examples.x: I remember playing hanetsuki with my cousins during New Year's celebrations last year.
She brought a colorful hagoita to the picnic, and we played hanetsuki all afternoon.
My grandmother taught me how to play hanetsuki when I was a child.
I had never heard of hanetsuki before, but it was easy to pick up and really enjoyable.
The kids were laughing and running around while playing hanetsuki in the backyard.

sense.definition: a traditional Japanese ball game in which players aim to keep a small ball in the air by kicking it without letting it touch the ground

examples.x: They played kemari in the park, trying to keep the ball in the air as long as possible.
During the festival, we watched a traditional kemari game performed by skilled players.
The children tried their hand at kemari, but they kept dropping the ball.
My grandfather told me stories about playing kemari with his friends when he was young.

sense.definition: a beach game played in Israel using wooden paddles to hit a small rubber ball back and forth between players without letting it touch the ground

examples.x: After a few rounds of matkot, we took a break to cool off in the ocean.
They played matkot for hours, laughing every time the ball hit the sand.
I love playing matkot with my friends whenever we go to the beach.
We brought our paddles and a ball for matkot, ready for some fun on the shore.

sense.definition: a traditional ball game played in Croatia, typically on the beach, where players try to keep a small ball in the air using their hands and feet

examples.x: They spent the afternoon playing picigin on the beach, laughing every time the ball almost hit the water.
Every summer, we gather with friends to play picigin along the Croatian coast.
The group of tourists tried picigin for the first time, struggling to keep the ball from touching the sea.
I watched as the locals played picigin in the shallow water, their movements quick and graceful.
He grew up playing picigin, and now he shows his friends how to play whenever they visit.

sense.definition: a traditional fairground game where players throw balls or objects at coconuts perched on stands in an attempt to knock them down

examples.x: We played a game of coconut shy at the carnival and won a stuffed animal.
I tried the coconut shy at the fair, but I kept missing the coconuts.
The kids were excited to see the coconut shy booth at the festival.
The coconut shy is always one of my favorite games at the summer fair.
He was disappointed when he couldn’t hit the coconuts at the coconut shy.

sense.definition: a drinking game where players throw ping pong balls across a table with the aim of landing them in cups of beer, and the opposing team has to drink the beer from the cups where the balls land

examples.x: We played beer pong at the party last night and it was so much fun.
Do you want to join us for a game of beer pong after dinner?
I’ve never been good at beer pong, but I still enjoy playing with friends.
He’s really good at beer pong—his shots are almost always on target.

sense.definition: a ball game similar to baseball, where players kick a rubber ball instead of hitting it with a bat

examples.x: We played a fun game of kickball at the family picnic yesterday.
The kids were excited to start their kickball game after school.
During recess, they always play kickball on the playground.

sense.definition: a game played on a table with two players, in which the players use special sticks to hit 16 numbered balls into the holes at the edge of the table

examples.x: Pool is a popular recreational activity enjoyed by people of all ages around the world.
Playing pool requires skill, strategy, and precision to accurately hit the balls into the pockets.
Pool tournaments attract skilled players who compete for prizes and prestige in various formats of the game.
The sound of balls clacking against each other and the smooth glide of the cue stick on the felt adds to the ambiance of a pool hall.

sense.definition: an arcade game in which players use flippers to hit a metal ball around a playfield, aiming to score points by hitting various targets and obstacles

examples.x: We took turns trying to keep the ball in play on the pinball machine.
The old diner had a classic pinball machine in the corner.
The pinball machine at the fair had so many cool features that we couldn't stop playing.
I spent hours playing the pinball machine at the arcade.

sense.definition: a lawn game where players throw two balls connected by a string at a ladder-like structure with three horizontal bars, aiming to wrap the bolas around the bars to score points

examples.x: We set up a ladder toss game in the backyard for the barbecue party.
The kids spent hours playing ladder toss at the family picnic.
I’m terrible at ladder toss, but I still enjoy playing it with my friends.
After dinner, we played a few rounds of ladder toss to relax.
The weather was perfect for a game of ladder toss on the beach.

sense.definition: a bat-and-ball game where players hit a ball and run around four bases to score points while the opposing team tries to stop them

examples.x: We played rounders in the park with friends last weekend.
Rounders is often a popular game at school sports days.
She hit the ball so hard in rounders that it flew across the field.
He was the last batter in rounders, and everyone was cheering for him to score.
I used to play rounders every summer when I was a kid.

sense.definition: a game that is played on grass and involves a series of hoops through which the players must roll wooden balls using hammer-like sticks called mallets

examples.x: The family set up a croquet course in the backyard for a fun afternoon activity.
During the picnic, they decided to organize a friendly croquet tournament.
She enjoys playing croquet with her friends on sunny weekends.
The park has a designated area for lawn games like croquet.

sense.definition: a playground game where four players stand in four squares drawn on the ground and take turns hitting a ball into each other's squares while following specific rules and patterns

examples.x: The kids spent their lunch break playing four square on the school playground.
Four square is a fun game that helps improve coordination and reflexes.
During recess, they argued over the rules of four square but soon agreed to play fairly.
Four square became the most popular game in the neighborhood during the summer holidays.

sense.definition: a traditional toy game where players attempt to catch a small ball with a cup attached to a string, testing hand-eye coordination and skill

examples.x: He bought a cup-and-ball as a souvenir during his trip to the old market.
The child spent hours practicing with her cup-and-ball toy, determined to catch the ball in the cup.
My grandmother told me she used to play with a wooden cup-and-ball when she was young.
During the family picnic, everyone took turns trying to master the cup-and-ball game.
